title: fix: update stale finance layout tests
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- 3 `layout.test.mjs` assertions broke when the Runway sheet was redesigned and were never updated (failing on `main`)
- Updated to current behavior: title `Runway 2026`; KPI shows running cash (anchor + net) and no longer includes a Burn field; header cols A/B are blank with month columns starting at col 2
- Tests-only — no code change. Full finance suite now green (47/47)
- Note: the title year in `layout.mjs` is hardcoded (`Runway 2026`) — worth making dynamic in a follow-up

gist.headline: Green across the board
gist.blurb: Updated our internal finance layout tests to match the new Runway sheet design. Everything is green and good to go!
gist.summary: Updated stale assertions in the finance layout tests to match the redesigned Runway sheet, bringing the test suite back to green.
gist.impact: Internal tests now correctly validate the current Runway sheet layout, ensuring reliable CI checks.
gist.visual_concept: The bee mascot checking off a green checklist or looking at a green glowing monitor (representing passing tests).
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of the cute bee mascot wearing tiny reading glasses, happily checking off a green checklist on a clipboard. A retro computer monitor in the background glows with green passing test logs. Soft lime green (#ecf874) and mint gradient background. Chunky retro sprites, warm lighting, lo-fi vibes like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
